shots.title: Fix broken test mocks
shots.notes: Two vi.mock() paths in next-action.test.ts and guards.test.ts didn't match import paths after package consolidation. Changed '../src/store.js' → '../../src/cli/store.js' and '../src/config.js' → '../../src/cli/config.js'. 20 tests fixed, CI green.

shots.title: Update version-bump script
shots.notes: Rewrote scripts/version-bump.mjs to bump single root package.json for @slope-dev/slope instead of looping over 5 old @srbryers/* packages.

shots.title: Create NPM granular access token

hazards.description: User first created a classic publish token (requires OTP) instead of a granular access token. Required a second attempt with the correct token type.

shots.notes: Manual step — replaced classic NPM publish token (EOTP error) with granular access token scoped to @slope-dev/slope. Updated NPM_TOKEN GitHub Actions secret. Took two attempts due to initial token type confusion.

shots.title: Clean up stale publish artifacts
shots.notes: Deleted failed v1.5.0-npm release and stale v1.5.0 release (GitHub Packages era) with their tags via gh CLI.

shots.title: Add CI check on PRs
shots.notes: Added pull_request trigger to .github/workflows/ci.yml. CI previously only ran on push to main.

shots.title: Test full release→publish flow

hazards.description: First publish attempt failed due to wrong token type from S29-3. Re-ran workflow after token was corrected — succeeded on second attempt.

shots.notes: Bumped version to 1.5.1, created GitHub Release v1.5.1, watched publish workflow succeed. Verified @slope-dev/slope@1.5.1 live on npm with provenance attestation.

conditions.description: Sprint was reactive — fixing breakage from recent consolidation refactor, not greenfield work
NPM token types and OIDC/provenance are under-documented — required trial and error for correct token configuration

nutrition.description: Full build + test + typecheck verified locally before every commit and confirmed green on CI
Commit-per-ticket discipline maintained — 5 code commits plus version bump, all pushed promptly
No new tests written — sprint fixed 20 existing broken tests. 1161 total tests passing.
Added .env to .gitignore immediately after user placed NPM token in local .env — prevented credential leak

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Fast, low-resistance sprint. All code fixes were surgical — 2-line mock path corrections, 13-line script rewrite, 2-line YAML addition. The only friction was external: NPM token types are confusing and the first token was the wrong type.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: When publishing scoped packages to npm from GitHub Actions: use a Granular Access Token (not Classic Publish, not Classic Automation). Classic tokens require OTP even in CI. Granular tokens bypass OTP and can be scoped to specific packages. The --provenance flag requires id-token: write permission in the workflow.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: The consolidation from 5 packages to 1 only broke 2 test files (mock paths), not the source code itself. The publish workflow was already correctly updated but the release was created from a stale commit that ran the old workflow.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: The release pipeline is now fully automated: bump version → push → create GitHub Release → auto-publish to npm with provenance. Future releases are a 3-step process.

bunker_locations: NPM classic publish tokens require OTP in CI — always use Granular Access Tokens for GitHub Actions
vi.mock() paths must exactly match the import paths used in the module under test — Vitest resolves them independently
Creating a GitHub Release from a stale commit runs the workflow version at that commit, not HEAD
yardage_book_updates: scripts/version-bump.mjs: now bumps single root package.json (was: loop over packages/*/package.json)
.github/workflows/ci.yml: triggers on push to main AND pull_request to main
.github/workflows/publish.yml: triggers on release published, uses npm publish --access public --provenance with granular access token
Release flow: node scripts/version-bump.mjs <ver> → commit + push → gh release create v<ver> → auto-publish
course_management_notes: 6 tickets, par 5, score 5 — clean par with 2 minor hazards (token type, publish retry) absorbed without penalties
Slope 1 confirmed appropriate — all small fixes and config changes, no new infrastructure
Sprint type: fix — reactive cleanup after consolidation refactor broke CI and publish pipeline
@slope-dev/slope@1.5.1 published to npm with provenance attestation — pipeline verified end-to-end
